assertions.reason: A fresh /tmp repo copy with only the `@enbox/agent` and `react-native-leveldb` Bun cache entries temporarily moved out of `~/.bun/install/cache` produced a cold-cache `bun install --frozen-lockfile` that exited 0 and logged `[postinstall] Patched ...` lines for both packages; all moved cache entries were restored afterward and the real repo `node_modules/` was untouched.

commands.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... copy repo to /tmp/patch-postinstall-logging-cold-cache-zu4h_y49/mobile excluding .git and node_modules; move @enbox/agent and react-native-leveldb cache entries to /tmp/patch-postinstall-logging-cold-cache-bun-cache-mvumforw; run bun install --frozen-lockfile; restore moved cache entries ... PY
commands.observation: Created an isolated temp repo, moved exactly four target Bun cache entries, ran `bun install --frozen-lockfile` successfully, recorded the log, and restored all four cache entries afterward.
Found both expected `react-native-leveldb` patch lines in the captured install log.
Found `@enbox/agent` patch lines in the captured install log, including `dist/types/enbox-user-agent.d.ts`.

frictions: Bun 1.3.11 reused previously postinstall-mutated cache entries until the four package-specific `@enbox/agent` and `react-native-leveldb` cache paths were temporarily moved out, matching the documented local cache gotcha.
